scenario: Helios Logistics, a carrier handling 45000 shipments per day, models a Camunda 8.8 receipt review process in Web Modeler. A developer must replace the initial Notify manager task with the SendGrid Email connector so the manager is notified about each uploaded receipt, using an API key obtained from a SendGrid account.
question: Which sequence does the docs prescribe for adding the SendGrid Email connector to the start event?

options.text: Open the BPMN XML in Web Modeler, edit the start event to set connectorRef to sendgrid, then save and redeploy the diagram so the API key and recipient fields appear in the right-hand panel
Add a generic service task next to the start event, then use the Change type menu to convert that task into a SendGrid Email connector before filling in the API key and email content

Click the start event to reveal the context pad, click Append connector, select the SendGrid Email connector option, then fill in the API key, sender, receiver, and email content in the properties panel

Drag a SendGrid Email shape from the connectors palette directly onto the start event so it overrides the existing task, then enter the API key and recipient in a dialog pop-up that opens automatically

a.text: Incorrect. The documented procedure stays inside the Web Modeler UI and uses the context pad and properties panel; it does not require editing the BPMN XML or setting a connectorRef attribute by hand.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs do not describe adding a generic service task first and then converting it; a connector is appended directly from the context pad of the existing element.

c.text: Correct. The docs describe clicking the start event to show the context pad, choosing Append connector, selecting the SendGrid Email connector option, and filling out the required information (API key, sender, receiver, email content) in the properties panel on the right.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no documented drag-and-drop palette overlay nor an auto-opening pop-up dialog; configuration happens in the right-hand properties panel of the newly appended task.

explanation: To add an out-of-the-box connector in Web Modeler, click the element to reveal its context pad, click Append connector, pick the desired connector (such as SendGrid Email), and complete the required fields in the properties panel on the right of the screen.

docs.title: Configuring an out-of-the-box connector - Add a connector
